summary: Good‑to‑great soundboard recording with crystal‑clear moments but plagued by audience mic chatter and occasional fuzzy guitar; the show shines on Ripple, Brown‑Eyed Women, All Over Now and a strong Wharf Rat while suffering from vocal slips and missing parts.
review: The performance captures an historic Warfield acoustic run with beautiful harmonies on Ripple and tight playing through the second set, highlighted by Brown‑Eyed Women, All Over Now and a soaring Wharf Rat. Recording quality is generally excellent and clear, though audience talk during “To Lay Me Down” and occasional fuzzy/high‑end guitar tone detract from an otherwise superb soundboard source.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance and overall clarity, offset by recurring complaints about talk‑overs, missing sections and occasional fuzzy guitar; weighted average yields a solid near‑4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear sound for most of the show, but includes intrusive audience mic chatter on “To Lay Me Down”, occasional fuzzy/high guitar tone, a high‑end heavy mix lacking bass, and short cuts/low‑quality audience patches in the first set.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Historic acoustic sets that set the tone, a tight and vocal‑rich second set with strong harmonies, followed by a serious third set featuring an introspective Garcia solo after Terrapin and an interesting Space segment.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Early equipment trouble left his guitar sounding pained/fuzzy; later his tone is high‑end and at times he sounds faint on “Rider" and has vocal issues on Bird Song.
band_member_comments.Bob: Stumbles vocally a couple of times during the acoustic set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Tight playing with a notable intro solo after Terrapin, solid drumming throughout.
